title: Speed Clone: GFP in 120 Seconds
description: Clone a GFP fragment directionally between KpnI and HindIII. No hints. The clock is running.

vector.name: pUC19
vector.sequence_hint: MCS contains KpnI (~407) and HindIII (~447). Both are unique cutters.

insert.name: GFP-2A-mCherry
insert.description: Bicistronic GFP-2A-mCherry cassette with KpnI (left) and HindIII (right) ends.

teaching_point: KpnI (3' GTAC overhang) and HindIII (5' AGCT overhang) have incompatible end types — perfect for directional cloning with zero self-ligation risk.

title: Mystery Enzyme Challenge
description: An insert has NotI ends (GGCC overhang). Identify the correct enzyme, cut the vector, perform ligation, and select the right antibiotic — all before time runs out.

vector.name: pBluescript SK+
vector.sequence_hint: This vector's MCS contains NotI, XbaI, SpeI, and BamHI. The insert has an 8-base recognition site — rare cutters leave large fragments intact.

insert.name: Genomic library clone
insert.description: A large genomic DNA fragment with NotI ends. NotI (GC^GGCCGC) is an 8-cutter — very rare in genomic DNA, ideal for large-insert cloning.

teaching_point: NotI recognises an 8-base sequence (GCGGCCGC) and therefore cuts genomic DNA very rarely (~1 site per 65 kb). This makes it ideal for cloning large genomic fragments without unwanted internal cuts.

title: Site-Destroyer Trap
description: A collaborator sends you a construct with SpeI ends, but your vector only has an XbaI site in the MCS. Under time pressure, decide whether to proceed and predict all ligation outcomes — including the hybrid site trap.

vector.name: pUC19
vector.sequence_hint: The pUC19 MCS has a single XbaI site at ~431 (TCTAGA). XbaI and SpeI share the CTAG overhang — but ligation destroys both sites.

insert.name: Fluorescent protein with SpeI ends
insert.description: mVenus coding sequence with SpeI (ACTAGT) sites on both ends. SpeI and XbaI are isoschizomers for ligation purposes (both generate 5'-CTAG), but the ligated product is not cut by either enzyme.

teaching_point: XbaI (T^CTAGA) and SpeI (A^CTAGT) leave identical CTAG 5' overhangs and can co-ligate. However, the ligation product (TCTAGTAGT) contains neither an XbaI nor a SpeI site — both are permanently destroyed. This is a critical consideration when planning multi-step cloning strategies.
